segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a young woman, the narrator, in a black Adidas tracksuit, smiling and waving from a well-lit room with string lights and framed art. She introduces her story in a clear, engaging voice, stating, "This is a story of how I raced my first half marathon," reflecting on how she "never thought I could, never thought I would, till I realized that I was created to run." An energetic, upbeat track begins as the scene transitions to dynamic, fast-paced clips of a marathon event, showing runners crossing a bridge at dusk, a woman joyfully raising her arms, and a close-up of a 21.1KM course map. The narrative then shifts to a personal preparation phase, with the same woman, now in athletic wear, on an outdoor track and later indoors. Text overlays like 'THE PREP' and 'LONG RUN DAY!' appear as she prepares for a long run, putting on white socks, lacing up her 'Adi Zero Boston 12 shoes' (which she explicitly states she'll use on race day), and adjusting her 'Garmin watch,' specifically the 'Approach S70,' noting it's meant for golf but also tracks other sports. The music continues as she explains, "Hi friendships, it is our long run day today," and that she needs to get ready. The video returns to her speaking directly to the camera in the initial setting, where she expresses her desire to share how she began running and this vlog about the Sydney Half Marathon.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with the Young Woman, dressed in a black Adidas tracksuit with white stripes, speaking directly to the camera in a warmly lit indoor setting with string lights. Her clear, enthusiastic voice, with a slight accent, introduces the topic: "the four things I did and you most definitely can do to believe that you yourself are a runner." She explains that to tell the story "truly and in totality," she needs to "take it back." Upbeat, light background music begins as she describes the initial "situation" during a "global pandemic" where she was "super lazy, very much not fit." She then introduces "Abby," who "eventually became my workout buddy." The scene transitions to outdoor footage labeled 'MAY OF 2021', showing the Young Woman running on a paved path at sunset, silhouetted against a golden sky. She narrates how, after a few weeks of training for fun, Abby "decides to impulsively sign up to the virtual Boston Marathon," specifically a "42K." The Young Woman, as a workout buddy, "signed up with her." A quick cut shows her in a car, reflected in the rearview mirror, looking determined, followed by her running outdoors in a park. Her voice, slightly muffled, announces "Here we go. Confirmation," followed by a humming sound. A montage shows her running on a treadmill, stretching, and preparing for a run. She excitedly recounts "Day one" of their training, describing it as "grueling," and mentions finishing "18K for the first time," with another voice (Abby's) interjecting "We're live" and "Oh my god." The Young Woman, now in a black sports bra and shorts, adjusts her white Garmin Approach S70 watch and laces up her white and black Adidas Boston 12s. She continues, stating the experience was "tiring" yet "fulfilling," acknowledging her body "was definitely not physically ready for this," with Abby's voice again exclaiming "Oh my god." The video returns to her indoors, where she discusses the book 'EAT & RUN BY SCOTT JUREK', explaining how she "brought together all of the books" and "gathered the mantras" she could say while running, quoting, "pain is inevitable, suffering is..." as the segment ends.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a Young Woman, wearing a black Adidas tracksuit with white stripes, seated in a softly lit room with sheer curtains and string lights. She speaks directly to the camera with a clear, expressive, and motivational voice, using emphatic hand gestures as she states, "I could do all things through Christ who strengthens me," a quote that also appears as on-screen text. The scene transitions to her in a black hoodie with a red and blue circular logo, sitting at a desk equipped with a computer monitor, microphone, hand sanitizer, and a pencil holder. She continues speaking, smiling and looking off-camera, with subtitles appearing as she advises, "it’s not a mind game, it’s something you’re having fun with." She adds, "That's right. But it's nice to have goals, kasi masarap naman 'yun eh," incorporating a Filipino phrase, and further encourages, "but don't be afraid of not making it, and the goal is to be happy with it." She elaborates, "Yes, you're right. Go, some experience, and to see also how far you can go." The video then cuts back to her in the Adidas tracksuit, where she gestures energetically, and a bold title card appears: "I. THINK LIKE A RUNNER." She continues her passionate speech, maintaining eye contact with the camera, explaining, "And she was right. I signed up to this thing for fun. Why am I not treating it as something I'm doing for fun? Who am I even trying to compete with? The belief that I do not have the capacity. Here, and now, I learned that in order for me to become a runner, I had to think like a runner." She then discusses her next race, stating, "So, this next time around, yes, it's a half marathon, but I wanted to do it at a quicker pace. I didn't want to just run. I wanted to run fast. And my confidence in setting this..." Her speech is filled with determination, underscored by her consistent hand motions, all within a modern, cozy home office environment featuring framed artwork and a large iMac.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with the Young Woman, dressed in her black Adidas tracksuit, seated in a cozy home office adorned with sheer curtains, string lights, a computer monitor, framed artwork, and various desk items. She speaks directly to the camera with expressive hand gestures and a warm smile, her voice motivational as she reflects on achieving her first marathon and the importance of holding onto past successes to build confidence for future challenges. She emphasizes, "whatever that challenge is in your life... if I did that, I can do this. Hold on to that thing... those things are very much necessary to help you in building the confidence to do hard things." A collage of past running moments, showing her in different athletic outfits, posing with friends, and celebrating a 6:15:47 race time, visually reinforces her narrative. Returning to the home office, she continues her enthusiastic dialogue, expressing how "It's so encouraging that after two and a bit years, Abby and I find ourselves in the same place, excited to run again, together." A 'BACK TO THE PRESENT' text overlay appears as the scene shifts to an outdoor evening setting. Here, the Young Woman, now in a black tank top, sits on a stone wall with her friend, Abby, both adjusting their hair in preparation for a run. The camera then captures a scenic dusk sky over a residential area before transitioning to a dark, moving shot of the Young Woman and Abby running at night, illuminated by headlamps and streetlights. The sounds of their heavy breathing and excited voices fill the audio, with one exclaiming, "Let's go, long run!" Another voice, out of breath but triumphant, declares, "You can't see us at all. We just went up a hill that we usually walk. Progress!" On-screen text defines 'LONG RUN DAY' as the furthest distance run at a comfortable pace. As they continue running on the dimly lit road, their headlamps casting shadows, Abby's voice is heard stating, "We're at nine... 9 kilometers. One hour," capturing the essence of their dedicated, late-night training session.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with the Young Woman, in a black Adidas tracksuit, speaking directly to the camera from her cozy home office. Her clear, articulate, and encouraging voice advises viewers to find a workout buddy, a team, or a coach to push their limits, promising they will discover a new love for running. Soft lighting from sheer curtains with string lights, a large iMac, framed artwork, a microphone, and hand sanitizer create a personalized backdrop. The scene transitions to a dimly lit gym where she, now in an orange sports bra and blue shorts, greets the viewer with a casual "Hello." She explains her "little workout setup" for an "easy run," clarifying that she does two such runs weekly, lasting 30 minutes to an hour, as on-screen text reads 'EASY DAY'. As she begins running on a treadmill, wearing white SHOKZ OPEN RUN PROS earbuds, the rhythmic sound of the treadmill accompanies her slightly more breathless voice. She announces she's testing her running playlist, which she describes as consisting of "a lot of 80s songs and songs that are 180 BPM." An on-screen overlay confirms her workout soundtrack includes tracks like 'Blitzkrieg Bop' and 'Don’t Stop Me Now'. After stopping her run, the Young Woman, still wearing her white Garmin Approach S70 watch, wipes sweat from her forehead and sits on the floor. Her voice, now less breathless, confirms she "just finished our easy run," a 30-minute session she was "very excited for... for the first time in a while," despite initially feeling "really reluctant." On-screen text reiterates that 'EASY DAYS happen twice a week for some recovery and more time on your feet!'

segments.integrated_caption: In a dimly lit gym, a Young Woman, wearing an orange sports bra, blue shorts, white SHOKZ OPEN RUN PROS earbuds, and a white Garmin Approach S70 watch, sits on a black yoga mat. She speaks directly to the camera, occasionally gesturing with her hands and touching her face, possibly to wipe sweat, as she reflects on her training journey. She explains her decision to run a 21km race instead of a full marathon, expressing her nervousness about the 2:30 cut-off time and the potential disappointment of not finishing or receiving a medal. She admits to being nervous about vlogging due to the fear of not completing the race and disappointing herself and others. However, she recounts how sticking to her consistent training, even on days she missed out, led to noticeable improvement. She proudly mentions a recent 17-kilometer long run, which she completed well, leaving her feeling happy, excited, and "pumped," though she cautions against becoming overconfident. The background reveals gym equipment, including a rack of dumbbells and resistance bands, reinforcing the fitness setting.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a dimly lit gym, where the Young Woman, visibly sweaty and wearing white SHOKZ OPEN RUN PROS earbuds and a white Garmin Approach S70 watch, speaks directly to the camera in a calm, reflective tone, stating, "I still need to be cautious, I still need to be disciplined, I still need to pray that the Lord sustains me because you never know what conditions may arise on the day itself." She then transitions into a forward fold yoga stretch on a black yoga mat, accompanied by gentle, ambient, almost meditative music and sounds of deep breathing, with on-screen text reading 'EASY DAYS yoga on easy days are the best things in the woooorld!'. She then says, "Okay, gonna go now." The scene shifts to a car interior, where the Young Woman, now in a blue hoodie, sits beside a smiling friend, with on-screen text announcing 'THE TRIP 3 DAYS TO THE HALF', all set to upbeat, soulful music featuring a female vocalist singing about "flying out" and "dropping me off." The journey continues to an airport terminal and check-in counter, where background chatter and ambient airport sounds become audible. The Young Woman is then seen walking outside a modern building, reflected in a circular mirror, before appearing in a warmly lit hotel lobby. The video culminates at a booth with a sign reading 'Nothing Feels Better than running Sydney', where the Young Woman signs a whiteboard, explaining amidst background chatter, "We have arrived at Sydney. So every time there's a marathon, there also is an expo, which is basically where you pick up your bib. And you get to meet people and we were actually invited um to the..." The segment concludes with a warm embrace between two men in the hotel lobby.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a warmly lit hotel lobby where the Young Woman, in a bright yellow top, speaks directly to the camera with expressive hand gestures. Her clear, conversational voice, carrying a slight accent, explains she's coached by someone who's been doing races with them since she was nine, expressing excitement for his long-term project. The scene cuts to a colorful 21.1KM Course Map poster, then to a bustling event booth with a 'Nothing Feels Better than running Sydney' sign, where the Young Woman, still audibly excited, signs a whiteboard with a red marker. Transitioning to a dimly lit hotel room at night, she sits by a window overlooking city lights, eating from a bowl with chopsticks. Her voice shifts to a more intimate, reflective tone as she details her evening race preps, including finishing her playlist and preparing for an 'acclimatization run' tomorrow morning to 'feel the weather, feel the terrain.' She sighs, stating her intention to 'relax,' and despite the hectic travel, repeatedly expresses her profound happiness to be there. Later, she's seen eating a slice of pizza from a cardboard box, her hair still damp, playfully asking, 'I mean, how can you not get excited?' before exclaiming, 'Oh, delicious pizza!' The final shot shows her lying in bed in a patterned pink pajama top, visibly anxious with on-screen text '*cue nerves kicking in*', as she utters a final, slightly strained 'Hey!'

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens in a dimly lit hotel room, where a Young Woman, wearing a pink, patterned pajama top, lies propped against a dark headboard, partially covered by white sheets. Her expression is tense and anxious, with eyes often closed or looking upward, and her hands frequently moving to her chest and face in distress, as on-screen text reads '*cue nerves kicking in*'. She mutters to herself, her body slightly trembling, explaining in a reflective and somewhat hesitant voice that "This is my first real run race." She contrasts it with a "virtual" 42K she did two years ago, which had "no setup, no people, no finish line." She recalls the last time she participated in a "real" race with an expo, banners, people, and a race bib, noting it was "so long ago," when she was "probably like 14, 15 years old, maybe 16 years old," calculating that it was "8 years ago." She concludes, "So naturally, I will be nervous," acknowledging her current state. She reflects that doing races back then "was clockwork," knowing her routine and mindset, but "this time around it's a little bit different because this is a goal I set for myself," emphasizing the personal significance rather than just joining her siblings. The low, moody lighting and quiet environment enhance her vulnerability and mental strain, capturing a raw moment of pre-event nerves.

segments.integrated_caption: In a dimly lit hotel room, the Young Woman, dressed in a pink, patterned pajama top, lies propped against a dark, textured headboard, her white sheets rumpled around her. Her pensive expression and restless movements, frequently touching her chest and face, visually convey her deep emotional distress and anxiety. As she reflects inward, her voice, matching her anxious demeanor, articulates a profound internal struggle. She speaks about the pressure of owning one's accomplishments, stating, "When an accomplishment that you want to achieve is your own, there is more pressure to get it done well," because "it's not imposed by anybody else." She emphasizes the need to "own it" with "your whole heart." This realization, she explains, is why she couldn't sleep, acknowledging her nervousness. She reflects on an upcoming "Sunday" event, asserting, "If I fail on Sunday, it's nobody else's fault but my own," and conversely, "if I do well on Sunday... it's a success that I can own." Her monologue continues as she recalls having lunch with "my Ninang Sally" a few days prior, who "reminded me..." The intimate, tense atmosphere of the room, with a visible doorway and wall-mounted thermostat in the background, underscores her solitary moment of processing these intense thoughts and feelings.

segments.integrated_caption: In a dimly lit hotel room, a Young Woman in a pink, patterned pajama top lies in bed with an anxious expression, frequently touching her chest and face. She speaks reflectively, stating, "That the Bible is God's letter to us... How beautiful is that? Like it's his word." She expresses happiness that the hotel room provides a Bible, noting her preference for a physical copy over a "Bible app on my phone." Reaching for a red book, she opens it and begins reading Psalm 5:11-12 aloud, her voice clear and focused, as the scripture appears on-screen. Her expression shifts to calm focus as she reads, "But let all who take refuge in you rejoice; let them ever sing for joy, and spread your protection over them, that those who love your name may exult in you. For you bless the righteous, O Lord; you cover him with favor as with a shield." After a deep breath, she reflects, "That all those who take refuge in him rejoice, and I have to take refuge in him." She then shifts to practical matters, stating, "Okay, I have to sleep now. We have an early run tomorrow morning," before softly bidding, "Good night. I'll see you tomorrow." She rises from bed, looking composedly at the camera, then pulls back sheer curtains to reveal a bright, urban cityscape. The scene transitions to her stepping out of a car onto a sidewalk, her bright turquoise running shoes visible, accompanied by the distinct sound of a car door closing and footsteps, signifying her transition from introspection to purposeful action.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with an upbeat, slightly electronic motivational track as dynamic urban scenes unfold, showing runners in motion on a paved walkway near modern buildings, with a digital sign clearly displaying 'SYDNEY MARATHON' and '17TH SEPTEMBER'. Close-ups of athletic shoes emphasize the movement, followed by a scenic view of the Sydney Harbour Bridge and people relaxing along the waterfront, reinforcing the city's vibrant atmosphere. The scene then shifts indoors to a hotel room where a young woman, identified as Maria by her race bib 'P1970 MARIA' for the 'Sydney Marathon Candidate Race,' is seen in patterned pajamas, writing on the bib. A female voice, later identified as the Camera Operator, asks, "What are you doing, Mom?" Maria, with earphones in, smiles and adjusts her hair, responding, "I'm writing prayers for people so that I can pray for them during the race." The Camera Operator replies, "You're so nice," followed by shared laughter. Maria then states, "is my seventh marathon," to which the Camera Operator exclaims, "Seventh marathon!" Maria adds, "Seventh, and Daddy's gonna pace me." The camera cuts to a man in a white t-shirt, stretching deliberately. A male voice, belonging to the man, playfully asks, "How many marathons have you done? How do we know that you're even trustworthy?" He then reveals, "This is my 13th marathon," prompting the Camera Operator to ask, "Really?" He chuckles, admitting, "Yeah, I didn't finish one. Because I paced mom then halfway through. Then I quit now, because I couldn't." The final shot shows a table with a Starbucks cup, 'Chewy' snacks, and other personal belongings, all set against the continued motivational music, conveying a tone of anticipation and personal readiness for the upcoming marathon.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens in a hotel room with the Man in White T-Shirt, identified as Anthony Pangilinan, lying on a bed, propped on pillows. He gestures animatedly while speaking in a calm, instructional tone about 'crying out to the Lord' and the significance of the last letter 'N' in 'MARATHON,' which he explains stands for 'Never rule out divine intervention.' The scene then shifts to the Young Woman, Hannah Pangilinan (Maria, as seen on her race bib), wearing a patterned pajama top and large clear-framed glasses, sitting on the same bed. She enthusiastically exclaims, 'Oh my gosh, yes! They have a new book!' as she proudly holds up 'Marriage Is a Marathon: Run It One Step at a Time' by Anthony Pangilinan & Maricel Laxa-Pangilinan. On-screen text reinforces the personal connection: 'MARRIAGE IS A MARATHON BY MY PARENTS <3'. Hannah explains that each letter of 'MARATHON' represents a chapter heading in 'our book,' detailing lessons learned from running marathons worldwide, including training, racing, and recovery. She hopes the book inspires viewers to run their first marathon. Anthony interjects with a playful 'Quiet, Dad' to someone off-screen, confirming his role as her father. Hannah then mentions a link in the description for purchasing the book before revealing her own pre-race preparations, stating she 'got FOMO' seeing others prepare and is now getting ready herself. The cluttered hotel room background, with a suitcase and race bibs, including one labeled 'P1970 MARIA' for the 'Sydney Marathon Candidate Race,' visually underscores her upcoming participation.

segments.integrated_caption: The video begins with a blurred shot of Hannah in her home office, wearing a black Adidas tracksuit with white stripes, which quickly sharpens as she sits in a black office chair. She speaks directly to the camera with animated expressions and expressive hand gestures, emphasizing her points. Behind her, a large iMac with a dark screen, a black desktop microphone, hand sanitizer, a pencil holder, and a small framed photo personalize her workspace, while sheer white curtains with warm string lights and two framed artworks (one abstract, one floral) create a cozy ambiance. Throughout, upbeat, non-lyrical background music plays. Hannah energetically discusses her running preparation, stating, "Number three, look like a runner." She shares her excitement for new gear, saying, "there's nothing that excites me more than having a new cute outfit to go outside and run," or trying a "new watch," "new headphones," or a "new playlist," adding that "building up the gear is just like so much fun for me every time I enter into a new hobby." She clarifies that viewers don't need to use the exact same items she does, but that adding to the 'getting ready process' "reinforces the identity that you are creating." She illustrates this by saying, "A runner wears this, a runner uses this, a runner listens to this, a runner looks like this." The segment transitions with a bold pink text overlay reading 'GETTING READY FOR A *HALF* MARATHON WITH HANNAH'. The scene then shifts to a hotel room where Hannah, now in a black long-sleeve top, leans over a bed and holds up her white race bib, clearly labeled 'P30078 HANNAH' for the Sydney Marathon. She greets viewers, "Hello guys and welcome to getting ready for a marathon with Hannah," and immediately points to the bib, stating, "First thing you need is a race bib because..."

Behind her, a large iMac with a dark screen sits on a desk, accompanied by a black desktop microphone, a bottle of hand sanitizer, a pencil holder with colored pencils, and a small framed photo, creating a personalized workspace. The background features sheer white curtains adorned with warm string lights, adding a cozy ambiance, and two framed pieces of artwork — one abstract and one floral — on the wall. She speaks energetically, using expressive hand movements, including pointing, clenching fists, and spreading her hands wide, conveying enthusiasm. The segment transitions with a text overlay reading 'GETTING READY FOR A *HALF* MARATHON WITH HANNAH' in bold pink letters, followed by a shot of her in a different setting — a hotel room — where she is now wearing a black long-sleeve top, leaning over a bed, and holding up a race bib labeled 'P30078 HANNAH' for the Sydney Marathon, indicating her preparation for the event. The shift from the home office to the hotel room marks a narrative progression from personal reflection to active race preparation.

segments.integrated_caption: In a warmly lit hotel room, a Young Woman in a black Adidas tracksuit enthusiastically unboxes her Sydney Marathon race gear, speaking directly to the camera with expressive gestures. On-screen text labels each item as she presents them. She begins by holding up her race bib, 'P30078 HANNAH', before showcasing orange-framed Rudy Project Spinhiled Shades, which she calls 'Rudy Project shades', noting they match her race belt and 'it doesn't move when I run' as she puts them on with a smile. Next, she displays white Adidas Boston 12s, referring to them as her 'beautiful and used Boston Adizeros.' She then presents a packet of 'Roctane electrolyte capsules' (GU Roctane Electrolyte Capsules), followed by her black Flipbelt, which she opens, explaining she'll put 'essentials, so gels' in it. She holds up red and green Squeezy Energy Gels, specifying a 'raspberry' one without caffeine and a 'lemon' one with caffeine. For audio, she displays a black case with Shokz OpenRun Pro bone-conduction earbuds, calling them 'my Shokz Open Run Pros' and exclaiming, 'Look at how beautiful this is and the color is stunning.' She concludes by showing a small tube of 'lip balm with SPF' (SPF 30). The neutral-toned hotel room background remains consistent, with another person partially visible.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a modern hotel room where Hannah, dressed in a black Adidas tracksuit, gestures as she speaks, explaining she'll put items inside her "race belt." The camera then focuses on her hand reaching for race gear on a bed, including a black and orange sports bra, black shorts, a black Flipbelt, and her Sydney Marathon race bib labeled 'P30078 HANNAH', which she refers to as the "color scheme for tomorrow." She picks up a pair of white Adidas Boston 12s, showcasing them as "beautiful white." Hannah then announces a "quick break" for an early dinner to allow for early sleep, as the scene transitions to a dining setup. A table covered with a white cloth is laden with food, red wine, water, and chocolates. Three people are seated: Hannah, a Man in White T-Shirt, and a Woman in Patterned Pajama Top. The Man in White T-Shirt leads a pre-meal prayer, saying "Amen, amen." The Woman in Patterned Pajama Top, gesturing, asks, "Can you tell the viewers why we run? Why do you run? Why do you torture yourself?" The Man in White T-Shirt, sipping red wine, humorously replies, "So I can enjoy wine," adding "True." Hannah then explains her running motivation: "Training for a marathon or any other race or just a run allows me to have my peace and quiet. I actually don't run with any music. So I'm able to just zone in to just hearing myself and then time of reflection with the Lord," concluding, "I really enjoy that." The Man in White T-Shirt then challenges Hannah, asking, "Why do you run, Hannah? You're not even supposed to run." Hannah responds with a questioning "Hmm?" as he continues, "It was never your intention to run this. You just..." before trailing off, capturing a moment of intimate conversation and shared meal in the cozy hotel room.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a dimly lit hotel room where the Young Woman, dressed in a pink patterned pajama set, methodically packs a silver suitcase placed on a folding table. She explains in a calm, clear voice that she's "setting it up here" with "things I don't want to have to think about," as she carefully places items. She holds up a Nature Valley Protein bar, identifying it as a "salted caramel protein one," and also packs a GU Roctane Electrolyte Capsule packet, occasionally glancing at her reflection in a large mirror. She then adds a black drawstring bag with a 'RAY' logo, mentioning she's including "an extra shirt in my bag just in case." As she continues, she lists her planned routine, stating she'll "wear this" (referring to her later Adidas tracksuit), "wear my shoes," and use "vitamins, put sunscreen, headphones." The camera briefly focuses on close-ups of her gear, including a black Adidas bag, black running shoes, a bottle of 'Best Buy Max 100% SPF' sunscreen, and a packet of 'Squeezy Energy Gel'. After finishing packing, she concludes, "I'm pretty much set," and sits contemplatively on the edge of the bed. The scene transitions to a well-lit home office where the Young Woman, now wearing a black Adidas tracksuit with white stripes, sits in front of a computer monitor. With a calm and engaging expression, she speaks directly to the camera, reflecting, "I will be the first to say that I love thinking and overthinking and planning and listing and journaling and overthinking that I forget to act." The background features sheer curtains with string lights, framed artwork, and a desk with various items including a microphone and hand sanitizer.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with the Young Woman, dressed in a black Adidas tracksuit with white stripes, seated in a softly lit home office, speaking directly to the camera with a calm and reflective demeanor. She shares, "You can think like a runner. You can surround yourself with runners. And you can look like one. But if you don't actually go out there, put one foot in front of the other and run, you will never truly understand what you were created to do." As her speech concludes, an upbeat song with male vocals begins, setting an energetic tone. The scene quickly transitions to her washing her face in a bathroom, then to a dimly lit hotel room where, now in a pink patterned pajama set, she packs a silver suitcase on a folding table with various race essentials, including a black drawstring bag, Nature Valley Protein Bars, Squeezy Energy Gel, Best Buy Max 100% SPF Sunscreen, and black running shoes. Next, she is seen in a red and black athletic top, putting on a white headband and a black backpack with a 'RUDY PROJECT' logo, before walking through a hotel corridor and descending a staircase. The visuals then shift to a nighttime cityscape featuring the illuminated Luna Park with its iconic face, followed by her walking along a bridge at dusk. At the race start line, she smiles, holding a red Squeezy Energy Gel packet, with on-screen text reading 'it's a guys race today but whatever i'm ready'. Her voice, now excited and overlaid with the continuing music, exclaims, "Good morning. It's race day. It's race hour. It's almost race hour. Whatever is in me right now..." The video culminates in a wide shot of runners, including the Young Woman wearing orange-framed Rudy Project Rydon Shades and gesturing with a peace sign, on a bridge at sunrise with the Sydney Harbour Bridge visible in the background, as a blue elevation graph overlay indicates the 21.1KM course, all set to the persistent energetic music.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a dynamic first-person perspective of the Young Woman, clad in a red and black athletic top with orange-framed Rudy Project Rydon Shades perched on her head and a white Garmin Approach S70 on her left wrist, running a marathon. Upbeat, energetic pop music with a female vocalist plays, featuring lyrics like "I'm gonna go, go, go, there's no stopping me." As she transitions from an urban street lined with buildings and orange traffic cones to a scenic, tree-lined park path bathed in golden light, the 21.1KM Course Map at the bottom of the screen tracks her progress towards the 10KM and then 15KM marks. She speaks directly to the camera, reflecting, "It's around the time you get comfortable with people taking over, I mean, overtaking you," a sentiment reinforced by the on-screen text "you get comfortable with people taking over." She continues, "Because everyone has different training, everyone has their own race," aligning with the text "because everyone has different training" and "run your own race." She advises, "Don't be trapped in the comparison cycle," which also appears as on-screen text, adding, "I'm trying not to, to be a part of that." She expresses, "I'm glad I just still, I'm on my own," and after a brief sigh, notes, "Just give me a kilometer. Coming into kilometer," as other Marathon Runners are visible around her, some wearing race bibs, with a white bus in the background. The music continues with lyrics about not stopping. Nearing the halfway point, she declares, "Almost halfway now," a sentiment echoed by the "almost halfway!" on-screen text. With renewed vigor, she states, "The music is turned on. That's how you know. We're gonna do this!" her voice filled with determination as the music swells, encapsulating a journey of personal resolve.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a dynamic, first-person perspective of the Young Woman, wearing orange-framed Rudy Project Rydon Shades, a black long-sleeve top over a red sports bra, black shorts, and a white Garmin Approach S70 on her left wrist, crossing the Sydney Marathon finish line. Her face beams with exhaustion and triumph as the on-screen timer reads '1:59:47!', marking her sub-2-hour half-marathon achievement. She is surrounded by cheering spectators behind barriers, with Asics banners lining the course and the Sydney cityscape in the background, all set to an upbeat, triumphant, wordless vocal music featuring a 'Ha-ra-ra' melody. The scene cuts to a split-screen montage emphasizing her effort and joy as she runs from multiple angles, followed by her radiant celebration receiving a medal with fellow runners. The scene then transitions to a dimly lit, cozy home office where the Young Woman, now in a black Adidas tracksuit with white stripes, sits before a large iMac, framed artwork, and sheer curtains adorned with warm string lights. She speaks directly to the camera with animated, reflective, and engaging expressions, stating, "And that is the vlog. I had such a beautiful time preparing and running this race. I consider this little video a love letter to running. I've learned so much becoming a runner. I look forward to all that it's gonna shape me to be still, running a race, beating distances. Even if you're tired, continuing on. We were all designed created to run. We were all designed created to move forward, to progress, to challenge ourselves and to find accomplishment." Her soft lighting highlights her face, creating a calm, conversational atmosphere as she shares her profound experience.

segments.integrated_caption: In a cozy, softly lit home office, a Young Woman wearing a black Adidas tracksuit with white stripes and the Adidas logo speaks directly to the camera with animated expressions and frequent hand gestures. Behind her, a large iMac, a black desktop microphone, and personalized desk items are visible, alongside sheer white curtains adorned with warm string lights and two framed artworks. She passionately discusses "beating those challenges" and introduces her "favorite verse ever," Hebrews 12:1-2, which appears as on-screen text. She reads the passage aloud, emphasizing themes of endurance and faith: "Therefore, since we are surrounded by so great a cloud of witnesses, let us lay down aside every weight and sin which clings so closely, and let us run with endurance, the race that is set before us, looking to Jesus, the founder and perfecter of our faith." She elaborates on this, stating, "Running is like life itself," urging listeners to "letting go of bad habits, of bad beliefs that you are not able, that you are not capable," and reiterating that the race was "already set before us by the creator himself." She expresses her love for the "picture of running alongside Jesus in this race that I call life," and how "running is what has been able to teach me to keep on going." Towards the end, she thanks "a lot of people for making this possible," specifically mentioning "Rudy Project for just gearing us up with the best possible shades," as the Rudy Project logo appears, and then "Adidas for clo-" as the Adidas logo also appears, before the audio cuts off. Her voice is clear, articulate, and full of conviction throughout her sermon-like delivery.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with the Young Woman, dressed in a black Adidas tracksuit with white stripes, seated in her home office. She speaks directly to the camera with animated expressions and frequent hand gestures, set against a backdrop of sheer curtains with string lights, a large iMac, framed artwork, and various desk items. She thanks Smart for their "functional, yet cute clothes" and for keeping them connected abroad with fast, easy-to-sign-up roaming services, calling it her "go-to definitely." On-screen text promoting Smart’s roaming pack with a URL appears. She then thanks Gatorade for keeping them "hydrated" and "electrolyted," humorously correcting her pronunciation of the word. Logos for G, Standard Insurance, and Runrio appear sequentially in the top left corner as she expresses gratitude to Standard Insurance for covering them with travel insurance, especially when "embarking an international race," highlighting the importance of being protected as an athlete and calling it a "safety net." The scene briefly cuts to a nighttime view of the Sydney Opera House and Harbour Bridge, illuminated with purple and white lights, with a woman waving in the foreground, followed by a daytime shot of two people walking hand-in-hand down a tree-lined sidewalk, visually representing the travel and race context. The video then returns to the Young Woman in her home office, continuing her direct address with a warm, engaging demeanor, her hands clasped together. She thanks Coach Rio from Runrio for making all these things possible and recounts having the "best experience being welcomed by the Sydney Marathon group." Finally, she thanks her parents, calling them "the reasons that I run" and emphasizing that "This race that we call life is not worth it without you guys running alongside me," and for "getting me up in the morning."

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a Young Woman, wearing a black Adidas tracksuit with white stripes on the sleeves and the Adidas logo on the chest, seated in a cozy home office. The background features sheer curtains with warm string lights, a large iMac with a dark screen, a black desktop microphone, and two framed pieces of artwork. Desk items like hand sanitizer, colored pencils, and a small framed photo are also visible. She speaks directly to the camera with an engaging, expressive voice and frequent animated hand gestures, discussing a marathon. She thanks supporters for the necessary aid to run the race and congratulates participants, specifically mentioning her dad who completed the full marathon, while she did the half. She recounts her dad pacing with her mom, who sadly didn't finish but ran an impressive 38 kilometers, realizing she lacked proper nutrition and preparation. The Young Woman commendably encourages her mom for getting back up and running again after the traumatic experience, using it as fuel for future races, and attributes the glory to God. Throughout this, white text overlay appears, showing the URL 'https://gigaroam.smart.com.ph/' for booking a roaming pack, which is later replaced by a black circular 'G' logo, a yellow 'Standard Insurance' logo, and a red 'Runrio' logo, all in the top left corner. Midway through, the scene transitions to a different room with beige curtains, a white couch, and a shelving unit with a camera and a hat. Here, she is now wearing a black zip-up long-sleeve top, which appears to be a hoodie with a red and blue circular logo on the left chest, continuing her expressive speech with hand movements. Her tone shifts to excited surprise as she exclaims, "Oh my god! I completely forgot! We are doing a giveaway!" The lighting remains soft and natural, maintaining a warm and intimate atmosphere across both settings.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a cozy, well-lit room with beige curtains and a shelving unit, where a Young Woman, wearing a black zip-up long-sleeve top, stands enthusiastically, holding a bundle of giveaway items. These include two green Gatorade water bottles, a white book titled 'Marriage Is a Marathon: Run It One Step at a Time', a black Adidas box, and a gray Garmin box. She speaks about shoe sizes and encourages viewers to go out and run, then exclaims, "What are the mechanics? We gotta talk about that!" On-screen text lists the six giveaway items: Gatorade Merch, Miam Book, Garmin Watch, Shokz Run Belt, Rudy Proj Shades, and Adidas Runners. The scene transitions to her sitting on a white couch, still in the same inviting room, as she explains the "super easy" giveaway mechanics. She instructs viewers to "share with me your name and your Instagram handle" and to "make sure that you are following my Instagram and of course, the Instagram of our sponsors." On-screen text updates sequentially to display these instructions. She continues, "the last thing is just talk to me," asking why they want to start or continue running and which of the four points from her video they'd like to apply. The on-screen text updates to reflect these prompts, including "Talk to me! Share why you want to start/continue your running journey & which of the 4 points you’d like to apply?" She then instructs, "Use the hashtag #TAKBOWITHHANNAH," which also appears on-screen, along with the announcement date: "ILL ANNOUNCE WINNER ON IG BY APRIL 1, 2024." She maintains eye contact with the camera, smiling and gesturing with energy, expressing her excitement to see entries and send the items. The Young Woman concludes with an excited, high-pitched exclamation, "I'm actually running a marathon in three weeks!" The background remains consistent, creating a warm and inviting atmosphere throughout.

She speaks clearly and engagingly, expressing nervousness about posting content, revealing an overtraining injury, and sharing her excitement for the upcoming Boston Marathon on April 15th, while also expressing gratitude and a desire to inspire her audience to run various distances.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of the Young Woman, seated on a white couch in a warmly lit room with beige curtains and a wooden shelving unit holding a camera and a hat. Wearing a black zip-up long-sleeve top, she speaks expressively to the camera, smiling and gesturing with her hands, stating in a friendly, appreciative tone, "if you're here still to the end, you're a real one, you're an OG." Her cheerful and engaging demeanor continues as she adds, "And yeah, I'll see you guys in my next video," raising both hands toward the camera in a playful, welcoming gesture before concluding with a distinct kiss sound and a "Bye." The scene then cuts to black before transitioning to an outdoor Track Field under a bright blue sky with scattered clouds. The red running track curves alongside a green grassy field, bordered by trees and a chain-link fence. Two women, including the Young Woman, are seen running along the track from a distance, moving away from the camera, accompanied by the distinct sounds of running and heavy, exerted breathing. The Young Woman is in a white sports bra and light-colored shorts, while the other runner wears a green top and dark shorts. The open and sunny environment, with stadium lights and a large black screen in the background, suggests a training or recreational running session in a public sports facility.
